denominator_used: N = 1 pathway row; definition: the single 'GCNP_SHH_UP_LATE.V1_UP' row within the 'Top 10 Pathways Overexpressed in MPNST-G1' table on the 'Fig 3c' sheet of 41467_2023_38432_MOESM10_ESM.xlsx
assumptions_made: The 'Normalized Enrichment Score' column in the MPNST-G1 block (rows 4-13) of the Fig 3c sheet corresponds to NES values; no transformation/rounding was applied to the cell value; the question refers to the MPNST-G1 block rather than the MPNST-G2 block (which does not contain GCNP_SHH_UP_LATE.V1_UP anyway).
step_by_step_analysis:
A. DATA LOADED
   - File: 41467_2023_38432_MOESM10_ESM.xlsx
   - Sheet: 'Fig 3c' (dimensions A1:J31)
   - Sheet contains two labeled blocks: 'Top 10 Pathways Overexpressed in MPNST-G1' (starting row 1) and 'Top 10 Pathways Overexpressed in MPNST-G2' (starting row 17).
   - Columns of interest: 'Pathways' (A) and 'Normalized Enrichment Score' (B).

B. COHORT DEFINITION
   - Filter 1: Restrict to MPNST-G1 block (rows 4-13) -> 10 pathways
   - Filter 2: Select pathway name == 'GCNP_SHH_UP_LATE.V1_UP' -> 1 row

C. VARIABLE CODING
   - Pathway name read from column A.
   - NES read from column B.

D. COMPUTATION
   - Loaded the workbook with openpyxl (data_only=True) and iterated rows of sheet 'Fig 3c'.
   - Located row with Pathways == 'GCNP_SHH_UP_LATE.V1_UP' within the MPNST-G1 block.
   - Read the Normalized Enrichment Score value from column B of that row.

E. RESULT
   - NES = 1.67699

F. CAVEATS
   - Value is reported to the full precision stored in the source cell (1.67699). Paper text/figure may round this (e.g., 1.68).
   - The same pathway name does not appear in the MPNST-G2 block, so there is no ambiguity.
